howItWorks.intro: This cat food calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Daily Calories = RER × Life Stage Factor
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How many calories does a cat need?
faq.answer: Average indoor adult cat: 200-250 calories/day. Active cats may need 300+. Kittens need more calories per pound for growth.

faq.question: Wet vs dry food?
faq.answer: Wet food provides hydration (important for cats who do not drink enough water) and is typically lower in carbs. Dry food is convenient and helps dental health. A mix is ideal.

faq.question: How often should I feed my cat?
faq.answer: Adult cats: 2 meals per day or measured free-feeding. Kittens: 3-4 meals per day. Avoid unlimited free-feeding as it leads to obesity.

faq.question: Is my cat overweight?
faq.answer: Over 50% of US cats are overweight. A healthy cat has a visible waist, you can feel ribs without pressing hard, and has minimal belly pouch.
